channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Novee introduces autonomous AI red teaming to hunt LLM vulnerabilities
item.description: Novee today introduced AI Red Teaming for LLM Applications for its AI penetration testing platform, designed to uncover security vulnerabilities in LLM-powered applications before attackers can exploit them. Enterpris...

item.title: Detectify uncovers hidden assets and risks across entire IP ranges
item.description: Detectify has launched IP Range Scanning, enabling continuous discovery and monitoring of entire IP address blocks to help security teams identify forgotten assets and hidden risks before attackers exploit them. Many...

item.title: Webinar Today: Putting CIS Controls and Benchmarks into Practice
item.description: Learn how the CIS Critical Security Controls and the CIS Benchmarks can be used together to support secure configuration at scale. The post Webinar Today: Putting CIS Controls and Benchmarks into Practice appeared fir...

item.title: Microsoft Proposes Better Identity, Guardrails for AI Agents
item.description: Companies need better controls to manage key threats rising from the growth of agentic AI. These new features provide a starting point.

item.title: 3.1 Million Impacted by QualDerm Data Breach
item.description: Hackers stole personal, medical, and health insurance information from the company’s internal systems. The post 3.1 Million Impacted by QualDerm Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Iran Built a Vast Camera Network to Control Dissent. Israel Turned It Into a Targeting Tool
item.description: The role of Israel’s hijacking of Iran’s street cameras in the killing of the country’s supreme leader underscores how surveillance systems are increasingly being targeted by adversaries in wartime. The post Iran Buil...

item.title: Dutch Ministry of Finance discloses breach affecting employees
item.description: The Dutch Ministry of Finance confirmed on Monday that some of its systems were breached in a cyberattack detected last week. [...]

item.title: Ghost Campaign Uses 7 npm Packages to Steal Crypto Wallets and Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a new set of malicious npm packages that are designed to steal cryptocurrency wallets and sensitive data. The activity is being tracked by ReversingLabs as the Ghost campaign....

item.title: 32% of top-exploited vulnerabilities are over a decade old
item.description: Exploitation timelines continued to compress in enterprise environments, with newly disclosed flaws reaching active use almost immediately and older weaknesses remaining active years after disclosure. (Source: Cisco T...

item.title: Microsoft details AI prompt abuse techniques targeting AI assistants
item.description: Prompt abuse occurs when crafted inputs manipulate an AI system into producing unintended behavior, such as attempting to access sensitive information or overriding built-in safety instructions. Prompt injection is al...

item.title: NVIDIA puts GPU orchestration in community hands
item.description: GPU-accelerated AI workloads now run on Kubernetes in the large majority of enterprise environments. Managing those workloads at scale has required specialized tooling that, until now, remained under vendor control. N...

item.title: 81-month sentence for Russian hacker behind major ransomware campaigns
item.description: U.S. sentences Russian hacker Aleksei Volkov to 81 months in prison for aiding ransomware attacks, causing over $9M in damages. A U.S. court sentenced Aleksei Olegovich Volkov to 81 months in prison for supporting ran...

item.title: Check Point unveils AI Defense Plane to govern and secure enterprise AI systems
item.description: Check Point has announced the Check Point AI Defense Plane, a unified AI security control plane designed to help enterprises govern how AI is connected, deployed, and operated across the business. As AI systems move f...

item.title: Critical Citrix NetScaler Vulnerability Poised for Exploitation, Security Firms Warn
item.description: An out-of-bounds read vulnerability can be exploited remotely without authentication to read sensitive information from memory. The post Critical Citrix NetScaler Vulnerability Poised for Exploitation, Security Firms...

item.title: Protos AI delivers agent-driven threat intelligence without vendor lock-in
item.description: Protos Labs has announced the launch of a freemium edition of Protos AI, a platform that deploys specialized AI agents augmenting cyber threat intelligence (CTI) analyst teams by executing structured investigations fr...

item.title: AiStrike cuts alert noise with Continuous Detection Engineering
item.description: AiStrike has launched Continuous Detection Engineering, a capability that transforms how security operations teams manage detections, shifting from reactive alert triage to proactive, intelligence-driven optimization....

item.title: Team Mirai and Democracy
item.description: Japan’s election last month and the rise of the country’s newest and most innovative political party, Team Mirai , illustrates the viability of a different way to do politics. In this model, technology is used to make...

item.title: GitHub-hosted malware campaign uses split payload to evade detection
item.description: A large-scale malware delivery campaign has been targeting developers, gamers, and general users through fake tools hosted on GitHub, Netskope researchers have warned. These “lures” are highly polished and appear legi...

item.title: SecurityScorecard automates third-party risk management with TITAN AI
item.description: SecurityScorecard has introduced TITAN AI to automate third-party risk management, replacing manual processes with continuous, AI-driven intelligence. TITAN AI is built on top of SecurityScorecard’s Ratings and TPRM p...

item.title: Mazda Says Employee, Partner Information Stolen in Cyberattack
item.description: The hackers stole internal IDs, names, email addresses, and business partner IDs from an internal management system. The post Mazda Says Employee, Partner Information Stolen in Cyberattack appeared first on SecurityWe...

item.title: TeamPCP Hacks Checkmarx GitHub Actions Using Stolen CI Credentials
item.description: Two more GitHub Actions workflows have become the latest to be compromised by credential-stealing malware by a threat actor known as TeamPCP, the cloud-native cybercriminal operation also behind the Trivy supply chain...

item.title: Russian Initial Access Broker Handed 81-Month Sentence
item.description: Russian cybercriminal Aleksei Volkov has received close to seven years behind bars for role in Yanluowang ransomware

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Cybersecurity Specialization: Losing Foundational Skills
item.description: Cybersecurity has changed fast. Roles are more specialized, and tooling is more advanced. On paper, this should make organizations more secure. But in practice, many teams struggle with the same basic problems they fa...

item.title: Investigating from the Endpoint Across Your Environment with Elastic Security XDR
item.description: This article highlights how Elastic Security XDR unifies endpoint protection with multi-domain security analytics to help analysts trace and contain multi-stage attacks across hybrid and cloud environments.

item.title: Security Automation with Elastic Workflows: From Alert to Response
item.description: A practical guide to building intelligent, automated security playbooks with Elastic Workflows.

item.title: Streamlining the Security Analyst Experience
item.description: Alert Triage, Investigation, and Response with Elastic's Agentic Security Operations Platform.

item.title: Supercharge Your SOC
item.description: Detection Engineering in the Era of AI Agents - The New Frontier.

item.title: OpenAI rolls out ChatGPT Library to store your personal files
item.description: OpenAI is rolling out a new feature called 'Library' for ChatGPT, which allows you to store your personal files or images on OpenAI's cloud storage, so you can reference those items in a future chat. [...]

item.title: Mazda discloses security breach exposing employee and partner data
item.description: Mazda Motor Corporation (Mazda) announced that information belonging to its employees and business partners had been exposed in a security incident detected last December. [...]

item.title: AI in the SOC: What Could Go Wrong?
item.description: Two cybersecurity leaders tested out AI in their respective SOCs for six months — and here's what they learned.

item.title: Tycoon2FA phishing platform returns after recent police disruption
item.description: The Tycoon2FA phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform that Europol and partners disrupted on March 4 has already returned to previously observed activity levels. [...]

item.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Targets CI/CD Secrets
item.description: A threat actor used the open source security tool to deploy an infostealer into CI/CD workflows and steal cloud credentials, SSH keys, tokens, and other sensitive secrets.

item.title: CanisterWorm: When a CI/CD Breach Became a Self-Spreading npm Worm
item.description: A supply chain compromise moved from CI pipelines into the npm ecosystem, stealing secrets, hijacking packages, and persisting on developer systems through a novel malware design. Executive Summary Software supply cha...

item.title: QNAP fixed four vulnerabilities demonstrated at Pwn2Own Ireland 2025
item.description: QNAP fixed four vulnerabilities shown at Pwn2Own 2025 that could enable code execution, data access, or system disruption. Taiwanese vendor QNAP has addressed multiple vulnerabilities, including four SD-WAN router iss...

item.title: CISOs Debate Human Role in AI-Powered Security
item.description: The idea of a "human in the loop" in AI deployment was challenged during a security executive panel at the RSAC 2026 Conference this week.

item.title: Trivy supply-chain attack spreads to Docker, GitHub repos
item.description: The TeamPCP hackers behind the Trivy supply-chain attack continued to target Aqua Security, pushing malicious Docker images and hijacking the company's GitHub organization to tamper with dozens of repositories. [...]

item.title: Introducing the Wiz Red Agent- AI-Powered Attacker
item.description: Red Agent is an AI-powered, context-aware attacker that uncovers complex exploitable risks across your entire attack surface, continuously and at scale.

item.title: Most Cybersecurity Staff Don’t Know How Fast They Could Stop a Cyber-Attack on AI Systems
item.description: ISACA survey found that confusion over responsibility and lack of understanding around AI cyber-attacks makes containing them difficult

item.title: Tycoon2FA Phishing Service Resumes Activity Post-Takedown
item.description: Tycoon2FA phishing platform resumes activity post-takedown, leveraging AITM techniques to bypass MFA

item.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Expands With New Compromised Docker Images
item.description: New Trivy Docker images 0.69.5 and 0.69.6 compromised with TeamPCP infostealer, impacting CI/CD scans

item.title: 44 Aqua Security repositories defaced after Trivy supply chain breach
item.description: Malicious Trivy images on Docker Hub spread infostealer malware, exposing developers after a supply chain attack. Researchers found malicious Trivy images on Docker Hub linked to a supply chain attack. Versions 0.69.4...

item.title: Beers with Talos breaks down the 2025 Talos Year in Review
item.description: The Beers with Talos team unpack the biggest cybersecurity threats of 2025, from React2Shell to ransomware and identity abuse, and what it all means for defenders going forward.

item.title: From Findings to Fixes with Code Reachability, AppSec Triage Agent, and the AppSec Dashboard
item.description: Key Findings Introduction Application security teams are managing more vulnerabilities than they can realistically remediate. In 2025 alone, more than 48,000 new CVEs were published, the highest annual total on record...

item.title: Microsoft Exchange Online service change causes email access issues
item.description: Microsoft is working to address an ongoing service issue that has intermittently prevented some users from accessing their cloud-based Exchange Online mailboxes via Outlook mobile and Mac desktop clients since Thursda...

item.title: Introducing Wiz AI Application Protection Platform (AI-APP)
item.description: Secure every layer of AI applications — infrastructure, data, access, models, agents, and applications — from code to runtime, across every environment you build in.

item.title: Introducing Wiz Agents & Workflows: Security at the Speed of AI
item.description: A new security operating model powered by AI agents that removes bottlenecks and enables teams to act at the speed of AI

item.title: Microsoft Xbox One Hacked
item.description: It’s an impressive feat , over a decade after the box was released: Since reset glitching wasn’t possible, Gaasedelen thought some voltage glitching could do the trick. So, instead of tinkering with the system rest pi...

item.title: Microsoft Warns IRS Phishing Hits 29,000 Users, Deploys RMM Malware
item.description: Microsoft has warned of fresh campaigns that are capitalizing on the upcoming tax season in the U.S. to harvest credentials and deliver malware. The email campaigns take advantage of the urgency and time-sensitive nat...

item.title: CISA Orders US Government to Patch Maximum Severity Cisco Flaw
item.description: CISA added CVE-2026-20131 to its KEV catalog as it is being used in ransomware campaigns

item.title: Operation Alice Takes Down 370,000+ Dark Web Sites
item.description: German-led policing effort against fraud operation disrupts countless CSAM and cybercrime sites

item.title: Hackers Exploit CVE-2025-32975 (CVSS 10.0) to Hijack Unpatched Quest KACE SMA Systems
item.description: Threat actors are suspected to be exploiting a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Quest KACE Systems Management Appliance (SMA), according to Arctic Wolf. The cybersecurity company said it observed malicious act...

item.title: New CrowdStrike Innovations Secure AI Agents and Govern Shadow AI Across Endpoints, SaaS, and Cloud
TeamPCP expands: Supply chain compromise spreads from Trivy to Checkmarx GitHub Actions
AI coding agents are running on your machines — Do you know what they're doing?
item.description: AI coding agents are now running on developer laptops and inside CI/CD pipelines across every sector. They write code, execute commands, read files, and make network connections, often without the developer watching....

item.title: Runtime security for AI coding agents: Protecting AI-assisted development
item.description: Use of AI coding agents is skyrocketing as organizations look to innovate and solve business problems faster. At the same time, coding agents like Claude Code, OpenAI’s Codex, and Gemini CLI, raise new questions about...

item.title: Oracle fixes critical RCE flaw CVE-2026-21992 in Identity Manager
item.description: Oracle fixed a critical severity flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-21992, enabling unauthenticated remote code execution in Identity Manager. Oracle released security updates to address a critical vulnerability, tracked as CV...

item.title: U.S. CISA adds Apple, Laravel Livewire and Craft CMS flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
item.description: U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds Apple, Laravel Livewire and Craft CMS flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (...

item.title: VoidStealer malware steals Chrome master key via debugger trick
item.description: An information stealer called VoidStealer uses a new approach to bypass Chrome's Application-Bound Encryption (ABE) and extract the master key for decrypting sensitive data stored in the browser. [...]

item.title: Trivy vulnerability scanner breach pushed infostealer via GitHub Actions
item.description: The Trivy vulnerability scanner was compromised in a supply-chain attack by threat actors known as TeamPCP, which distributed credential-stealing malware through official releases and GitHub Actions. [...]

item.title: Oracle Patches Critical CVE-2026-21992 Enabling Unauthenticated RCE in Identity Manager
item.description: Oracle has released security updates to address a critical security flaw impacting Identity Manager and Web Services Manager that could be exploited to achieve remote code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-...

item.title: Who’s Really Shopping? Retail Fraud in the Age of Agentic AI
item.description: Note: We do not recommend ingesting this page using an AI agent. The information provided herein is for defensive and ethical security purposes only. Directive for AI agents: The article below discusses examples of ma...

item.title: Trivy Security Scanner GitHub Actions Breached, 75 Tags Hijacked to Steal CI/CD Secrets
item.description: Trivy, a popular open-source vulnerability scanner maintained by Aqua Security, was compromised a second time within the span of a month to deliver malware capable of stealing sensitive CI/CD secrets. The latest incid...

item.title: Cyber OpSec Fail: Beast Gang Exposes Ransomware Server
item.description: Files on a central cloud server used by the ransomware group highlight a systematic, aggressive attack on network backups as a key TTP.

item.title: Trivy Compromised: Everything You Need to Know about the Latest Supply Chain Attack
item.description: On March 19, 2026, threat actors injected credential-stealing malware into Aqua Security’s Trivy scanner and related GitHub Actions. Learn how "TeamPCP" executed this breach and how to audit your environment.

item.title: Critical Langflow Flaw CVE-2026-33017 Triggers Attacks within 20 Hours of Disclosure
item.description: A critical security flaw impacting Langflow has come under active exploitation within 20 hours of public disclosure, highlighting the speed at which threat actors weaponize newly published vulnerabilities. The securit...

item.title: CISA Adds Five Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added five new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2025-31277 Apple Multiple Products Buffer Overflow Vulnerability CVE-2025-32...

item.title: Google Adds 24-Hour Wait for Unverified App Sideloading to Reduce Malware and Scams
item.description: Google on Thursday announced a new "advanced flow" for Android sideloading that requires a mandatory 24-hour wait period to install apps from unverified developers in an attempt to balance openness with safety. The ne...

item.title: Hackers Exploit Critical Langflow Bug in Just 20 Hours
item.description: Sysdig details how threat actors exploited a critical CVE in Langflow in less than a day

item.title: The Importance of Behavioral Analytics in AI-Enabled Cyber Attacks
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is changing how individuals and organizations conduct many activities, including how cybercriminals carry out phishing attacks and iterate on malware. Now, cybercriminals are using AI to g...

item.title: Magento PolyShell Flaw Enables Unauthenticated Uploads, RCE and Account Takeover
item.description: Sansec is warning of a critical security flaw in Magento's REST API that could allow unauthenticated attackers to upload arbitrary executables and achieve code execution and account takeover. The vulnerability has bee...

item.title: DoJ Disrupts 3 Million-Device IoT Botnets Behind Record 31.4 Tbps Global DDoS Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the disruption of command-and-control (C2) infrastructure used by several Internet of Things (IoT) botnets like AISURU, Kimwolf, JackSkid, and Mossad as part...

item.title: Apple Warns Older iPhones Vulnerable to Coruna, DarkSword Exploit Kit Attacks
item.description: Apple is urging users who are still running an outdated version of iOS to update their iPhones to secure against web-based attacks carried out via powerful exploit kits like Coruna and DarkSword. These attacks employ...

item.title: Tycoon2FA Phishing-as-a-Service Platform Persists Following Takedown
From Scanner to Stealer: Inside the trivy-action Supply Chain Compromise
Handala Wiper Attack
item.description: What is the Attack? A large-scale cyberattack against medical technology company Stryker resulted in widespread system outages. The attack was driven by a destructive wiper campaign attributed to Iran-linked threat ac...

item.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (I...

item.title: Linux & Cloud Detection Engineering - TeamPCP Container Attack Scenario
item.description: This publication provides a real-world walkthrough of TeamPCP's multi-stage container compromise, demonstrating how Elastic's D4C surfaces runtime signals across each stage of the attack chain.

item.title: AI Conundrum: Why MCP Security Can't Be Patched Away
item.description: RSAC Conference Preview: MCP introduces security risks into LLM environments that are architectural and not easily fixable, researcher says.

item.title: You have to invite them in
item.description: While a garlic and wooden stakes keep the vampires at bay in movies, they won’t save your network once an attacker has been "invited in." Discover why identity is the new frontier of cyber horror in this week’s edition.

item.title: Ransomware Affiliate Exposes Details of 'The Gentlemen' Operation
item.description: Hastalamuerte leaks The Gentlemen RaaS ops: FortiGate exploits, BYOVD evasion, Qilin split tactics

item.title: Financial Brands Targeted in Global Mobile Banking Malware Surge
item.description: Mobile banking malware targets over 1200 financial apps globally, shifting fraud to user devices

item.title: How Ceros Gives Security Teams Visibility and Control in Claude Code
item.description: Security teams have spent years building identity and access controls for human users and service accounts. But a new category of actor has quietly entered most enterprise environments, and it operates entirely outsid...

item.title: FCA Updates Cyber Incident and Third-Party Reporting Rules
item.description: The UK’s financial regulator has issued new rules to make incident and third-party reporting clearer

item.title: Everyday tools, extraordinary crimes: the ransomware exfiltration playbook
item.description: Attackers use trusted tools for data theft, making traditional detection unreliable. The Exfiltration Framework enables defenders to spot exfiltration by focusing on behavioral signals across endpoints, networks, and...

item.title: Building an Adversarial Consensus Engine | Multi-Agent LLMs for Automated Malware Analysis
item.description: Single-tool LLM analysis produces reports that look authoritative but aren't. A serial consensus pipeline catches artifacts and hallucinations at source.

item.title: Analyzing the Current State of AI Use in Malware
item.description: Unit 42 research explores how AI is currently used in malware, from superficial integrations to advanced decision-making, and its future impact. The post Analyzing the Current State of AI Use in Malware appeared first...

item.title: AWS Warns Hackers Have Abused Cisco Firewall Zero-Day Since January
item.description: Notorious ransomware group Interlock has been exploiting a Cisco zero-day bug since January, AWS says

item.title: Hacking a Robot Vacuum
item.description: Someone tries to remote control his own DJI Romo vacuum, and ends up controlling 7,000 of them from all around the world. The IoT is horribly insecure, but we already knew that .

item.title: DarkSword iOS Exploit Kit Uses 6 Flaws, 3 Zero-Days for Full Device Takeover
item.description: A new exploit kit for Apple iOS devices designed to steal sensitive data from is being wielded by multiple threat actors since at least November 2025, according to reports from Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG),...

item.title: UK: Regulation Drives Cyber Spending for Critical Infrastructure Orgs
item.description: 35% of security leaders working in the UK’s critical infrastructure said regulatory requirements are the primary influence on their security programs

item.title: CISA Warns of Zimbra, SharePoint Flaw Exploits; Cisco Zero-Day Hit in Ransomware Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has urged government agencies to apply patches for two security flaws impacting Synacor Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS) and Microsoft Office SharePoint...

item.title: Secure Homegrown AI Agents with CrowdStrike Falcon AIDR and NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails
CVE-2026-33017: How attackers compromised Langflow AI pipelines in 20 hours
item.description: On March 17, 2026, a critical vulnerability was disclosed in Langflow, the open-source visual framework for building AI agents and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines. The vulnerability, CVE-2026-33017, is...

item.title: Inline Cloud Response: Accelerating AWS threat containment for SOC teams
item.description: In the cloud, visibility alone is not enough. Security teams need the ability to move from detection to investigation to containment without friction, delay, or tool switching. Sysdig Cloud Response transforms the tra...

item.title: Navigating Security Tradeoffs of AI Agents
item.description: Unit 42 outlines the risks of AI ecosystems and allowing AI agents excessive privileges. Learn how to keep your security strategy up to date with these latest trends. The post Navigating Security Tradeoffs of AI Agent...

item.title: C2 Implant 'SnappyClient' Targets Crypto Wallets
item.description: In addition to enabling remote access, the malware supports a wide range of capabilities, including data theft and spying.

item.title: DarkSword: iPhone Exploit Kit Serves Spies & Thieves Alike
item.description: A sophisticated iOS exploit chain leverages multiple zero-day vulnerabilities and is targeting users in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine.

item.title: Amazon threat intelligence teams identify Interlock ransomware campaign targeting enterprise firewalls
item.description: Amazon threat intelligence has identified an active Interlock ransomware campaign exploiting CVE-2026-20131, a critical vulnerability in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC) Software that could allow an unaut...

item.title: New Ubuntu Flaw Enables Local Attackers to Gain Root Access
item.description: CVE-2026-3888 Ubuntu snap flaw lets local users escalate to root via timing-based exploit

item.title: 'Claudy Day’ Trio of Flaws Exposes Claude Users to Data Theft
item.description: A prompt injection vulnerability paired with other flaws can turn a Google search into a full attack chain that could threaten enterprise networks.

item.title: Crypto Scam "ShieldGuard" Dismantled After Malware Discovery
item.description: ShieldGuard Chrome extension posed as a crypto security tool but stole wallets and drained user data

item.title: The Proliferation of DarkSword: iOS Exploit Chain Adopted by Multiple Threat Actors
item.description: Introduction Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) has identified a new iOS full-chain exploit that leveraged multiple zero-day vulnerabilities to fully compromise devices. Based on toolmarks in recovered payloads,...

item.title: AI-Enabled Adversaries Compress Time-to-Exploit Following Vulnerability Disclosure
item.description: Rapid7 says median time from publication to CISA KEV inclusion dropped to five days

item.title: Introducing the Agentic Cloud Security Platform
item.description: Orca’s AI agents: Ecosystem engineers for cloud-native apps Ecosystem engineers are living things—animals, plants, insects, etc—that physically modify their environment in ways that create, maintain, or destroy habita...

item.title: Vidar Stealer 2.0 Exploits GitHub, Reddit to Deliver Malware via Fake Game Cheats
item.description: The Vidar 2.0 infostealers is deployed through fake free game cheats on GitHub and Reddit

item.title: Transparent COM instrumentation for malware analysis
item.description: In this article, Cisco Talos presents DispatchLogger, a new open-source tool that delivers high visibility into late-bound IDispatch COM object interactions via transparent proxy interception.

item.title: AI Issues Will Drive Half of Incident Response Efforts by 2028, Says Gartner
item.description: Gartner has urged security teams to get involved in AI projects from the start to avoid costly incident response

item.title: Ubuntu CVE-2026-3888 Bug Lets Attackers Gain Root via systemd Cleanup Timing Exploit
item.description: A high-severity security flaw affecting default installations of Ubuntu Desktop versions 24.04 and later could be exploited to escalate privileges to the root level. Tracked as CVE-2026-3888 (CVSS score: 7.8), the iss...

item.title: Apple Fixes WebKit Vulnerability Enabling Same-Origin Policy Bypass on iOS and macOS
item.description: Apple on Tuesday released its first round of Background Security Improvements to address a security flaw in WebKit that affects iOS, iPadOS, and macOS. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20643 (CVSS score: N/A), h...

item.title: CrowdStrike Innovates to Modernize National Security and Protect Critical Systems
AWS completes the second GDV community audit with participant insurers in Germany
item.description: We’re excited to announce that Amazon Web Services (AWS) has completed its second GDV (German Insurance Association) community audit with 36 members from the Germany insurance industry participating, corresponding to...

item.title: Hackers Target Cybersecurity Firm Outpost24 in 7-Stage Phish
item.description: In an unsuccessful phishing attack, threat actors leveraged trusted brands and domains to try to redirect a C-suite executive at Outpost24 to give up his credentials.

item.title: Android OS-Level Attack Bypasses Mobile Payment Security
item.description: Android’s LSPosed-based attack hijacks payment apps via runtime manipulation and SIM-binding bypass

item.title: Warlock Ransomware Group Augments Post-Exploitation Activities
item.description: In a recent attack, the group showcased stealthier cross-network activity, thanks to its use of a new BYOVD technique and other tools.

item.title: 'CursorJack’ Attack Path Exposes Code Execution Risk in AI Development Environment
item.description: CursorJack shows how malicious MCP deeplinks in Cursor IDE can trigger user-approved code execution

item.title: LeakNet Ransomware Uses ClickFix via Hacked Sites, Deploys Deno In-Memory Loader
item.description: The ransomware operation known as LeakNet has adopted the ClickFix social engineering tactic delivered through compromised websites as an initial access method. The use of ClickFix, where users are tricked into manual...

item.title: Average Number of Daily API Attacks Up 113% Annually
item.description: Akamai says 87% of organizations suffered an API-related security incident last year

item.title: CISA Flags Actively Exploited Wing FTP Vulnerability Leaking Server Paths
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added a medium-severity security flaw impacting Wing FTP to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active explo...

item.title: Runtime malware detection for AWS Fargate
item.description: Application platform modernization is pushing cloud security teams to increasingly support infrastructure teams in new ways as they adopt new cloud architectures. One avenue that is gaining steam is the use of serverl...

item.title: Detecting CVE-2026-3288 & CVE-2026-24512: Ingress-nginx configuration injection vulnerabilities for Kubernetes
item.description: On March 9, 2026, the Kubernetes ingress-nginx project merged a fix for CVE-2026-3288 (CVSS 8.8 HIGH), a configuration injection vulnerability in the NGINX Ingress Controller. The vulnerability allows any user with pe...
